title: Wasted Love
content:
What shall be done for sorrow
    With love whose race is run?
    Where help is none to borrow,
    What shall be done?

    In vain his hands have spun
    The web, or drawn the furrow:
    No rest their toil hath won.

    His task is all gone thorough,
    And fruit thereof is none:
    And who dare say to-morrow
    What shall be done?
meaning: About: Algernon Charles Swinburne (April 5, 1837 � April 10, 1909) was a Victorian era English poet. His poetry was highly controversial in its day, much of it containing recurring themes of sadomasochism, death-wish, lesbianism and irreligion.
